safety_warnings: This plan is not a guarantee of recovery.
Do not try to bypass MFA, guess passwords, phish, or access accounts without authorization.
hardening_steps: Generate and safely store new backup codes.
Add a passkey where the service supports it.
Update recovery email and phone number.
Store recovery details in a trusted password manager.
Document business admin access and recovery contacts.

gmail_mfa_loss.title: Lost MFA device for a Google account
gmail_mfa_loss.support_summary: I lost access to my authenticator app for a Google account and need help through the official recovery flow.
checklist: Try backup codes before any other recovery path.
Check whether a trusted device is still signed in to the Google account.
Use the Google account recovery flow instead of repeated sign-in attempts.
Prepare device history, recovery email access, and account timeline details.
Wait for official recovery checkpoints instead of spamming the form.
ownership_evidence: Trusted device details and recent sign-in context.
Recovery email still under your control.
Recent Google subscription or billing records.
common_mistakes: Submitting too many recovery attempts in a short period.
Trying random old passwords after the account starts challenging recovery.
Ignoring a trusted signed-in device that can confirm identity faster.
source_notes: Google recovery is strongest when the user stays consistent with devices, networks, and the official recovery flow.
Backup codes and existing trusted sessions should be used before more expensive recovery paths.

apple_trusted_device_loss.title: Lost trusted device or trusted phone for Apple ID recovery
apple_trusted_device_loss.support_summary: I lost access to a trusted Apple device or trusted phone number and need to recover access through Apple's official process.
checklist: Check whether any trusted Apple device is still signed in.
Try trusted phone or recovery email paths before starting a full account recovery wait period.
Use the official Apple recovery page and keep the same recovery context during review.
Prepare purchase, device, and account ownership details.
Do not remove old trusted numbers or devices until access is restored.
ownership_evidence: Trusted phone details if still under your control.
Recovery email still under your control.
Proof of Apple device or account ownership.
common_mistakes: Starting account recovery before checking a still-signed-in trusted Apple device.
Changing account details mid-recovery and resetting the trust signals.
Removing a trusted phone before the account is stable again.

official_links.label: Apple Support: If you forgot your Apple Account password

source_notes: Apple recovery often depends on trusted-device continuity and can involve waiting periods.
Proof tied to real Apple hardware and billing can strengthen the recovery path.

meta_account_hacked.title: Facebook or Instagram account hacked
meta_account_hacked.support_summary: The account appears compromised and I need to secure it through Meta's official hacked-account recovery flows.
checklist: Stop repeated login attempts that may make the account look more suspicious.
Use the official hacked-account flow for Facebook or Instagram.
Check whether the attacker changed the email, phone, or profile details.
Prepare identity evidence before opening additional support requests.
After recovery, change the password and revoke suspicious sessions.
ownership_evidence: Recovery email or phone details you still control.
Photo ID if the service requests it.
Recent profile details, usernames, and linked account history.
Business page, creator account, or ad account context if relevant.
common_mistakes: Not checking whether the recovery email or phone was changed by the attacker.
Opening too many duplicate reports instead of following one official path.
Delaying password reset and session revocation after recovery.
source_notes: Meta recovery often hinges on whether the original email or phone is still controlled by the owner.
Business and creator accounts need extra attention because compromise can spread into linked assets.

microsoft_admin_lockout.title: Microsoft admin or workspace lockout
microsoft_admin_lockout.support_summary: I am trying to restore admin or workspace access through Microsoft's official business recovery procedures.
checklist: Check whether another global admin still has working access.
Use the official Microsoft account recovery or business support path.
Prepare domain, tenant, billing, and admin history evidence.
Stop repeated sign-in attempts that can extend lockout or trigger extra review.
After recovery, create or verify break-glass admin access.
ownership_evidence: Backup admin details if another admin exists.
Known admin history and recent sign-in context.
common_mistakes: Treating a tenant admin lockout like a normal personal account reset.
Not checking whether another admin can recover the tenant faster.
Waiting until after the incident to create break-glass admin coverage.
source_notes: Business recovery is stronger when domain ownership and billing evidence are ready early.
Admin lockout needs business-grade recovery steps, not just consumer password reset behavior.

lost_mfa_device.support_summary: I lost access to my authenticator app and need to use the official recovery flow.
checklist: Try backup codes before any other recovery path.
Check whether a trusted device is still signed in.
Use the official account recovery page for the service.
Prepare identity, billing, device, or account ownership evidence.
Avoid repeated random attempts that can increase lockout risk.
evidence: Recovery email or recovery phone still under your control.

changed_phone_number.support_summary: My recovery phone number changed, and I need to recover access through official alternatives.
checklist: Try recovery email before requesting manual review.
Check trusted device recovery options.
Update the phone number only after access is restored.
Prepare proof that the account and old phone number belonged to you.
evidence: Recovery email still under your control.
Old and new phone number context.

suspicious_activity_lock.support_summary: My account was locked after suspicious activity, and I need to prove ownership safely.
checklist: Stop repeated login attempts that may extend the lockout.
Use the official locked-account or account recovery flow.
Review recent sign-in activity if any trusted device is still logged in.
Prepare ownership evidence before contacting support.
After recovery, change the password and revoke suspicious sessions.
evidence: Recent sign-in attempts you do not recognize.
